root.narration: The puck is dropped and the game is starting! You need to skate up the ice to join the play. How do you skate?

choices.text: Bend your knees, lean forward a little, and push off to the side with each stride
Stand up tall and take small steps forward
Kick your feet straight back like you are running

outcome.headline: Smooth skating!
outcome.explanation: By bending your knees and pushing to the side, you glide fast and stay balanced. This is the proper skating stride that all hockey players use.
outcome.whatToRemember: Always bend your knees when skating. Push your skates to the side, not straight back. A lower stance means more power and better balance.

keyTerms.definition: The way hockey players push their skates out to the side and glide forward on the ice

outcome.headline: You made it, but slowly
outcome.explanation: Standing tall makes it harder to balance and you cannot push off as hard. You got up the ice, but the other players beat you there.
outcome.whatToRemember: Bend your knees more to go faster. The lower you get, the more power you have in each push.

outcome.headline: Wipeout!
outcome.explanation: Kicking straight back does not work on ice like it does when running. Your skates slipped and you fell down. Hockey skating is different from running.
outcome.whatToRemember: On ice, you push to the side, not straight back. Think of it like pushing a scooter, but with your feet going sideways.
